title: Bitcoin Stamps Indexer
description: Rules for understanding and working with the Bitcoin Stamps indexer component.

patterns.description: Core block processing pipeline that orchestrates the entire indexing process
instructions: The Block Processor is the main orchestrator of the indexing process.
It manages chain reorganization, error recovery, and coordinates the processing pipeline.
The `filter_block_transactions` function uses the Rust parser to efficiently filter transactions.
Refer to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md for the complete data flow.

patterns.description: Processes and validates stamp data (base layer for all protocols)
instructions: StampProcessor handles data extraction and validation for all stamp types.
Supports both direct Bitcoin and Counterparty transaction sources.
For protocol specifications, check docs/PROTOCOLS.md.

patterns.description: SRC-20 token implementation (fungible tokens on Bitcoin)
instructions: Handles deploy, mint, and transfer operations for SRC-20 tokens.
Direct Bitcoin transactions are the standard after block 796000.
Counterparty SRC-20 transactions are no longer processed after block 796000.
Handles registration, transfer, and renewal of domain names.
Implements strict validation rules for domain registration.
Use parameterized queries with %s placeholders to prevent SQL injection.
Follow connection patterns from the file to ensure proper connection management.
Always use transactions for data consistency.
Always close connections to return them to the pool.
Check docs/DATABASE.md for complete database schema.

patterns.description: Counterparty API interaction for retrieving transaction data
instructions: Manages requests to the Counterparty API for pre-decoded transaction data.
Handles connection management, retries, and error recovery.
Classic stamps via Counterparty are still supported.
SRC-20 tokens via Counterparty are no longer processed after block 796000.

conceptDefinitions.definition: Main component that orchestrates the indexing process, managing block acquisition, transaction filtering, and protocol processing.
Component that orchestrates filtering of relevant transactions using the Rust parser and handles Counterparty issuances.
Components that validate and process protocol-specific operations (Stamp, SRC-20, SRC-101, etc.).
Process of handling blockchain reorganizations by reverting changes and reprocessing blocks.

relatedDocumentation.description: Overall system architecture and component relationships
Detailed protocol specifications and transaction sources
